name: pytest
summary: The most popular Python testing framework — simple, powerful, extensible

story.problem: Python's built-in unittest requires verbose boilerplate: test classes that inherit TestCase, self.assertEqual() calls, setUp/tearDown methods. Writing tests feels like work rather than just writing functions.
story.mental_model: A pytest test is just a function whose name starts with test_. Use plain assert statements — pytest rewrites them to show exactly what values didn't match on failure. Fixtures are functions that provide test data or resources, declared as parameters.
story.when_to_use: All Python testing: unit tests, integration tests, API tests, parametrized test suites. The de facto standard for Python projects of any size.
story.when_not_to_use: Rarely a reason not to use it. unittest is fine for tiny scripts. For browser automation, combine pytest with playwright or selenium.

alternatives.name: unittest
alternatives.reason: Standard library, no install needed, but more verbose

alternatives.name: hypothesis
alternatives.reason: Property-based testing — combines with pytest to generate test cases automatically

alternatives.name: ward
alternatives.reason: Modern pytest alternative with a cleaner output format

nodes.label: pytest
nodes.summary: CLI runner and test discovery engine. Run `pytest` to discover and run all tests.

nodes.label: @pytest.fixture
nodes.summary: Declare reusable setup/teardown. Request by name as a test function parameter.

nodes.label: @pytest.mark.*
nodes.summary: Tag tests: skip, xfail, parametrize, or custom marks for filtering.

nodes.label: @pytest.mark.parametrize
nodes.summary: Run the same test with multiple input/output pairs. One decorator, many test cases.

nodes.label: pytest.raises()
nodes.summary: Assert that a block of code raises a specific exception.

nodes.label: monkeypatch (fixture)
nodes.summary: Temporarily replace attributes, env vars, or functions for a single test.

nodes.label: conftest.py
nodes.summary: Special file where shared fixtures are defined. Automatically discovered by pytest.

nodes.label: tmp_path (fixture)
nodes.summary: Built-in fixture providing a temporary directory unique to the test invocation.

tasks.title: Write and run your first test

steps.label: Write test file
Run
steps.explanation: pytest discovers files matching test_*.py and functions matching test_*. -v shows each test name.

steps.label: Use in test
steps.explanation: pytest injects the fixture by matching the parameter name. The fixture runs fresh for each test by default.

steps.label: Parametrize
steps.explanation: This generates 4 separate test cases. Each shows individually in output.

steps.label: raises context
Patch env var
Patch a function
steps.code:
def test_no_network(monkeypatch):
    monkeypatch.setattr('requests.get',
        lambda url, **kw: FakeResponse())
    result = fetch_data('https://api.example.com')
    assert result is not None
steps.explanation: monkeypatch reverts all changes after the test automatically — no cleanup needed.
